title: Sprig Moulding with Air-Dry Clay
notes: Making a shallow one-piece sprig mould from a found object (button, shell, coin), pressing air-dry clay into it to produce a relief decoration, releasing the sprig, and applying it to a slab tile with score-and-slip. No kiln required. Produces a small decorative relief element that can be used to embellish a tile, box lid, or wall plaque.
